---
title: "Headers and footers"
format: html
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Headers and footers}
  %\VignetteEngine{quarto::html}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

This guide shows common recipes for configuring headers and footers in docstyle documents. Each recipe shows the YAML or QMD markup and explains what it produces.

For the full list of configuration options, see the [YAML reference](reference-yaml-schema.html).

## Simple footer

Add a footer with text on the left and a page number on the right:

```yaml
# _quarto.yml
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    left: "My Document"
    right: "Page {page}"
```

This produces a three-position footer with "My Document" on the left and "Page 1", "Page 2", etc. on the right. The centre position is empty.

You can also place content in any combination of positions:

```yaml
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    center: "Draft — Not for distribution"
```

### Single-position shorthand

For a single-position footer, use the `content` and `align` keys:

```yaml
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    content: "Page {page}"
    align: center
```

This is equivalent to setting `center: "Page {page}"` and leaving `left` and `right` empty.

## Suppress footer on title page

Set `first-page: false` to hide the footer on the document's first page:

```yaml
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    first-page: false
    left: "My Document"
    right: "Page {page} of {pages}"
```

The first page shows no footer. All subsequent pages show the configured footer. This uses Word's `w:titlePg` mechanism — the first page of the first section is treated as a title page.

## Page X of Y

Use `{page}` for the current page number and `{pages}` for the total document page count:

```yaml
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    right: "Page {page} of {pages}"
```

This renders as "Page 1 of 5", "Page 2 of 5", etc. The values are Word field codes (`PAGE` and `NUMPAGES`) that update automatically when the document is opened.

### Available placeholders

| Placeholder | Field code | Description |
|-------------|------------|-------------|
| `{page}` | `PAGE` | Current page number |
| `{pages}` | `NUMPAGES` | Total pages in the document |
| `{section-pages}` | `SECTIONPAGES` | Total pages in the current section |
| `{title}` | — | Document title (static text) |
| `{date}` | — | Current date (static text) |

## Per-section footer overrides

Section divs can override footer content for specific sections of the document. Attributes on the div override the YAML defaults; unspecified positions inherit from the previous section.

```markdown
::: {.section-body}
Body content inherits the YAML footer defaults.
:::

::: {.section-appendix footer-left="Appendix" footer-right="Page {page}"}
Appendix content with a different footer.
:::
```

In this example, the body section uses whatever footer is configured in `_quarto.yml`. The appendix section overrides the left position to say "Appendix" and the right position to show just the page number. The centre position inherits from the previous section.

### Cascade rules

Footer positions follow a "same as previous" cascade:

1. The first section inherits from the YAML defaults in `_quarto.yml`
2. Each subsequent section inherits from the previous section
3. Explicit div attributes (`footer-left`, `footer-right`, `footer-center`) override specific positions
4. Unspecified positions carry forward from the previous section

This mirrors Word's built-in "same as previous" behaviour for headers and footers.

## Page number restart

To restart page numbering in a section (e.g., an appendix starting at page 1), use `page-start` with `page-break="true"`:

```markdown
::: {.section-appendix page-break="true" page-start="1"
    footer-left="Appendix" footer-right="Page {page} of {section-pages}"}
Appendix content starts on a new page with page numbering at 1.
:::
```

**Important:** `page-start` requires `page-break="true"`. Page number restart does not work with continuous section breaks because Word cannot reset page numbering without a page break boundary.

### Section page counts with `{section-pages}`

When you restart page numbering, `{pages}` (NUMPAGES) still counts the entire document. Use `{section-pages}` (SECTIONPAGES) to count only the pages in the current section:

| Scenario | Use | Result on a 2-page appendix in a 5-page doc |
|----------|-----|----------------------------------------------|
| Total document pages | `{pages}` | "of 5" on every page |
| Pages in this section | `{section-pages}` | "of 2" on appendix pages |

A typical pattern:

- Body: `Page {page} of {pages}` — "Page 2 of 5"
- Appendix with `page-start="1"`: `Page {page} of {section-pages}` — "Page 1 of 2"

## Suppress footer in a section

To remove the footer from a specific section, set `footer="false"` on the div:
```markdown
::: {.section-references footer="false"}
References with no footer.
:::
```

This creates an empty footer for that section. Subsequent sections inherit the empty footer unless they specify their own.

## Headers

Headers use the same configuration pattern as footers. Replace `footer` with `header` in both YAML and div attributes:

```yaml
# _quarto.yml
docstyle:
  header:
    enabled: true
    first-page: false
    left: "My Document"
    right: "Draft"
```

Per-section overrides use `header-left`, `header-center`, and `header-right`:

```markdown
::: {.section-appendix header-left="Appendix" header-right=""}
Appendix with a different header.
:::
```

To suppress the header in a section, use `header="false"`:

```markdown
::: {.section-appendix header="false"}
Appendix with no header.
:::
```

Headers and footers are independent — you can configure one without the other, and per-section overrides for headers do not affect footers.

## Complete example

This example combines several recipes into a realistic document structure. A working version is available in `tests/projects/footer-mvp/`.

```yaml
# _quarto.yml
docstyle:
  footer:
    enabled: true
    first-page: false
    left: "My Report"
    right: "Page {page} of {pages}"
```

```markdown
---
title: "Annual Report"
---

Title page content (no footer due to first-page: false).

::: {.section-body}
## Introduction

Body content with default footer:
"My Report" on the left, "Page 2 of 5" on the right.
:::

::: {.section-appendix page-break="true" page-start="1"
    footer-left="Appendix" footer-right="Page {page} of {section-pages}"}
## Appendix A

Appendix with restarted numbering:
"Appendix" on the left, "Page 1 of 2" on the right.
:::
```

## Constraints and limitations

- **Tab stop positions** are currently hardcoded for US Letter with 1-inch margins (centre at 4680 twips, right at 9360 twips). Custom page sizes may produce misaligned footers.
- **`page-start` requires `page-break="true"`** — Word cannot restart page numbering within a continuous section.
- **`{section-pages}` requires `page-break="true"`** — SECTIONPAGES counts pages between page breaks. Without a page break, it counts the entire span of continuous sections.
- **Field code updates** — docstyle injects `<w:updateFields>` into settings.xml to force Word to recalculate field values on open. If page numbers appear stale, press Ctrl+A then F9 to manually refresh.
- **Pandoc strips footer references** from the reference.docx template during rendering. The docstyle finisher re-injects them in post-render, which is why footers require the docstyle pipeline (not plain `format: docx`).
